message:
audit: workspace unsafe_code overrides — fcp-sandbox is the only "allow" and is sound

Workspace policy: Cargo.toml:331 sets `unsafe_code = "deny"` as the
default for [workspace.lints.rust]. Two crates override:

  crates/fcp-host/Cargo.toml:12      → "forbid"  (stricter: blocks
                                                    `#[allow(...)]` escape
                                                    hatches; sound for the
                                                    gateway)
  crates/fcp-sandbox/Cargo.toml:13   → "allow"   (FFI to platform
                                                    sandbox APIs)

fcp-sandbox is the only crate where unsafe code lives in production
paths. Inventoried 37 unsafe sites across linux.rs (12), macos.rs (9),
windows.rs (16). Every block is an FFI call into a documented OS API:
libc::{setrlimit,prctl,syscall(SYS_landlock_*)} on Linux, sandbox_init
/sandbox_free_error on macOS, the Win32 job-object + integrity-level
+ SID family on Windows.

Soundness checks (each verified by inspection):
- FFI pointer lifetimes: every pointer passed is a stack-local that
  lives for the call duration, or a heap allocation paired with
  explicit cleanup (AllocateAndInitializeSid→FreeSid, OpenProcessToken
  →CloseHandle, CreateJobObjectW→CloseHandle in Drop, sandbox_init
  error buffer→sandbox_free_error).
- pre_exec async-signal-safety: linux.rs:649 and macos.rs:361
  correctly avoid allocation and logging inside the post-fork-pre-exec
  callback. Vec::with_capacity for the Landlock cpath buffers happens
  in the parent before pre_exec; inside, only c_path.as_ptr() is read.
  All inner calls (setrlimit, prctl, open, close, syscall) are
  async-signal-safe. Comment at linux.rs:651 documents the constraint.
- unsafe impl Send/Sync for WindowsSandbox (windows.rs:107,110): SAFETY
  comment justifies HANDLE values are opaque kernel IDs, never
  dereferenced as pointers — sound.
- Null/error checks: CStr::from_ptr(errorbuf) at macos.rs:327 is
  gated on the is_null() check three lines up; Win32 handle returns
  checked against is_null()/FALSE before use.
- unsafe extern blocks declare documented Apple SBPL / MSDN signatures
  with correct types.

One stylistic-only observation worth flagging for future maintainers
(not a memory safety issue, not patched):

  windows.rs:263-269 calls SetTokenInformation with
    length = sizeof(TOKEN_MANDATORY_LABEL) + GetLengthSid(sid)
  while the buffer at &label is a stack-local TOKEN_MANDATORY_LABEL
  (16 bytes on x86_64) and the SID is separately heap-allocated via
  AllocateAndInitializeSid. The kernel reaches the SID via the
  Label.Sid pointer (functionally correct — the heap SID is valid for
  its full GetLengthSid bytes), but the +GetLengthSid pattern is
  conventionally used when the SID is appended INLINE to the same
  buffer. The size argument over-states what's at &label. A future
  maintainer could either (a) allocate a single buffer with the SID
  inline and update Label.Sid to point inside it, or (b) drop the
  +GetLengthSid term. Both forms work; no fix this commit.

No exploitable findings. Workspace unsafe_code surface is small,
justified, and sound.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
